page.title: About the Calculators
page.subtitle: Learn about each health metric, the formula behind it, and how to interpret your results.

items.title: BMI Calculator
items.tagline: Are you at a healthy weight for your height?
items.shortDesc: Check if your weight is in a healthy range using the WHO international standard classification.
items.importance: BMI is the most widely used screening tool by doctors, dietitians, and public health professionals worldwide. As of 2022, WHO data shows 2.5 billion adults (43%) are overweight and 890 million (16%) are living with obesity — numbers that have more than doubled since 1990. Knowing your BMI is the first step in identifying potential weight-related health risks such as type 2 diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease.

items.title: BMR Calculator
items.tagline: How many calories does your body burn doing nothing?
items.shortDesc: Discover the calories your body burns at complete rest — the foundation of all calorie planning.
items.importance: Your BMR is the baseline for every nutrition and fitness plan. Whether you want to lose weight, gain muscle, or maintain your current body, understanding how many calories your body needs at rest allows you to make precise dietary adjustments and avoid under- or over-eating.

items.title: IBW Calculator
items.tagline: What is your medically recommended weight?
items.shortDesc: Find the medically recommended weight for your height using the Devine formula.
items.importance: Ideal Body Weight is used extensively in clinical medicine for calculating medication dosages, ventilator settings, and nutritional requirements. For fitness purposes, it provides a realistic weight target rooted in science rather than social trends or aesthetics.

items.title: Body Fat Calculator
items.tagline: What percentage of your body is fat?
items.shortDesc: Estimate your body fat percentage using simple measurements — no equipment needed.
items.importance: Body fat percentage is a far more meaningful measure of health and fitness than weight or BMI alone. Tracking it helps you understand whether you are losing fat vs. muscle, assess cardiovascular risk, and fine-tune your training and diet program for real body composition changes.

items.title: TDEE Calculator
items.tagline: How many calories do you need each day to maintain your weight?
items.shortDesc: Calculate your total daily calorie burn based on your activity level to manage weight precisely.
items.importance: TDEE is the single most practical number for managing your weight. It tells you exactly how many calories you burn every day — at rest and through activity. Use it to create a precise calorie surplus for muscle gain, a deficit for fat loss, or maintenance for weight stability.

items.title: Calorie Intake Calculator
items.tagline: How many calories does your body actually need each day?
items.shortDesc: Get your personalised daily calorie target for weight loss, maintenance, or gain — with macros included.
items.importance: Calories are the single most important variable in any weight management goal. WHO identifies energy imbalance as the primary driver of the global obesity epidemic. Knowing your exact daily calorie target removes guesswork and gives you a precise, evidence-based number to work from.

items.title: Carbohydrate Calculator
items.tagline: How many carbs should you eat per day?
items.shortDesc: Get a daily carbohydrate target in grams based on calorie needs, activity, and WHO-aligned carb ratios.
items.importance: Carbohydrate targeting helps align energy availability with your goal and training load. It supports better performance, appetite control, and long-term nutrition adherence when paired with overall calorie planning.

items.title: Weight Gain Calculator
items.fullTitle: Healthy Weight Gain — Calorie Surplus Planning
items.tagline: How many calories do you need to gain weight effectively?
items.shortDesc: Set your daily calorie surplus, estimate timeline to target weight, and get practical macros for healthy gain.
items.importance: Structured weight gain prevents the common mistake of gaining mostly body fat. Using a controlled surplus with realistic timelines helps improve lean mass outcomes, recovery, and long-term body composition quality.

items.title: Calories Burned Calculator
items.tagline: How many calories do you burn in a workout session?
items.shortDesc: Estimate calories burned from activity type, duration, and body weight using MET-based open standards.
items.importance: Exercise calorie-burn estimates help connect your workouts to measurable energy expenditure. Used correctly, this improves weight-management planning, helps avoid overestimating workout impact, and supports consistent long-term progress.

items.title: Body Type Calculator
items.tagline: What is your body shape — and what does it mean for your health?
items.shortDesc: Find your body shape and WHO-classified waist-to-hip ratio to understand your cardiovascular risk profile.
items.importance: Your body shape is a clinically recognised indicator of where your body stores fat and how that affects your health. WHO research shows central fat accumulation (apple shape) is more strongly linked to cardiovascular disease and type 2 diabetes than overall weight. Understanding your shape gives a risk signal that BMI alone cannot provide.

items.title: Protein Intake Calculator
items.fullTitle: Daily Protein Intake — WHO / FAO / UNU Aligned
items.tagline: How much protein does your body actually need each day?
items.shortDesc: Get a daily protein target in grams from your body weight and goal, based on WHO/FAO/UNU safe intake levels.
items.importance: Protein is essential for building and repairing muscle tissue, producing enzymes and hormones, and supporting immune function. WHO/FAO/UNU TRS 935 establishes the minimum safe intake at 0.83 g/kg/day — getting it right protects muscle mass, supports recovery, and prevents deficiency conditions including sarcopenia and kwashiorkor.

items.title: Water Intake Calculator
items.fullTitle: Daily Water Intake — EFSA / IOM Reference Values
items.tagline: How much water does your body need every day?
items.shortDesc: Get your personalised daily water target in litres and ml based on body weight and activity level.
items.importance: Water is the most essential nutrient — even mild dehydration of 1–2% of body weight impairs cognitive performance, physical output, and kidney function. EFSA and IOM reference values establish adequate intake at 2.0–2.5 L/day from beverages, adjusted upward for activity and climate.
